prompt: We need to migrate our primary users table from a single MySQL database to PostgreSQL. The table has 12 million rows and is read/written by 3 services: the main API (Node.js), a background job processor (Python/Celery), and an admin dashboard (Rails). We need zero downtime during the migration. The table has foreign key relationships with orders, sessions, and audit_logs tables. We're on AWS and currently use RDS MySQL 8.0. Target is RDS PostgreSQL 15. We have no existing PostgreSQL infrastructure.

assertions.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, and Execution Waves
Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
Every task card includes at minimum: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands
Each task card has a single primary purpose — no card combines unrelated concerns
Migration work is split into separate cards for: schema setup, dual-write/compatibility, data sync/backfill, read cutover, and cleanup
High-risk migration cards include explicit rollback or rollback notes
The decomposition accounts for all 3 services (Node.js API, Python/Celery, Rails admin) needing updates
The decomposition explicitly addresses the zero-downtime requirement with a staged approach
Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section (even if 'None')
